methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Brimstone is a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Cody Finke and Hugo Leandri. The company has raised over $100M to date. The sector comp universe comprises nine private firms and one public company (Lanza Technologies, ticker LNZA), including peers such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method. Using the $100M total raised as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of progression from Series A to the subsequent funding round yields an implied valuation of $200M. A peer-median approach is not supportable: while nine same-sector comparables exist, none satisfy narrow peer filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier), falling below the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable triangulation. Broadening to all-sector peers would introduce material methodological bias.

The triangulation range is $170M to $230M, with a median of $200M, supported by the single applicable method. Key uncertainties center on the lack of narrowly comparable stage and valuation precedents within the climate and energy sector, limiting confidence in stage-progression assumptions. Additional disclosure of revenue trajectory, patent position, and technology maturity would strengthen conviction in this range.
